full_cramming/pythia_160m.notes: Full cramming with fixed token budget. compressed_tokens is an input parameter (= max_sequence_length); only information_gain_bits and final_convergence are measured outputs.

full_cramming/llama_3_2_1b.notes: Full cramming Llama-3.2-1B baseline. Paper reports accuracy 0.998 ± 0 — near-perfect reconstruction; refactor should reproduce this within machine epsilon. Std=0 in paper, set to 0.001 to avoid div-by-zero in z-score; verdict relies on absolute |delta| < 0.005.

progressive/smollm2_135m.notes: Progressive cramming SmolLM2-135M baseline (lr=0.1, no alignment, no low-dim). compressed_tokens here is a MEASURED outcome — the largest stage_seq_len at which the trainer reaches 100% teacher-forced accuracy. analyze.py aggregates per-stage rows into one final-converged-stage row per sample before computing statistics. Trajectory Length (178 ± 40) and PCA 99% (12.2 ± 2.96) from Table 13 require post-hoc analysis over the saved stage trajectory and are added as a separate analyzer in a follow-up.

trajectory/smollm2_135m_progressive.paper_section: Table 13 (Appendix F), columns Trajectory Length + PCA 99%
trajectory/smollm2_135m_progressive.notes: Post-hoc trajectory analysis over the saved progressive_prefixes/ — no re-train. Trajectory Length = Σ ||e^(k+1) - e^(k)||_2 (paper eq. 3); PCA 99% = min number of PCA components reaching 99% cumulative explained variance, computed per sample and then averaged. Verdict uses the standard 2σ z-score gate from analyze.py.

attention_hijacking/smollm2_135m_progres.notes: Attention-hijacking analysis on the embeddings produced by the SmolLM2-135M progressive run. Paper Table 3 reports stats only for SmolLM2-1.7B (and a few other sizes); the 135M row is NOT in the paper, so we treat the comparison as qualitative — the verdict is determined by the `qualitative` block (compression_mass ≥ 30% AND correlation ≥ 0.5), confirming the hijacking phenomenon described in Section 5.5 also manifests at the 135M scale.
